hero.title: Navigate Your
hero.subtitle: The world's most trusted preventive healthcare navigation platform. Understand your risks. Plan your screenings. Access the right care — powered by evidence-based medicine and designed for every person on Earth.

modules.title: Your Complete Health Navigation Platform
modules.subtitle: Ten integrated modules, designed to work together. From risk awareness to care access — every step of your health journey, covered.

risk.title: Risk Assessment
risk.desc: Validated calculators for cardiovascular disease, diabetes, cancer, and mental health. Get your personalized 10-year risk with actionable steps.

screening.title: Screening Planner
screening.desc: Personalized screening schedules based on Thailand's national health guidelines. Know exactly what tests you need, when, and where.

symptoms.title: Symptom Navigator
symptoms.desc: Educational guidance to understand your symptoms, know when to seek care, and what questions to ask. Never a diagnosis — always empowerment.

providers.title: Find Providers
providers.desc: Locate verified hospitals, clinics, and screening centers near you. Quality ratings, insurance coverage, languages spoken.

diseases.title: Disease Library
diseases.desc: 10,000+ conditions covered with evidence-graded information. Every fact sourced, reviewed by medical professionals, and regularly updated.

academy.title: Health Academy
academy.desc: Evidence-based health education courses, videos, and interactive learning. Earn certificates. Improve your health literacy.

ai.title: AI Health Guide
ai.desc: Ask health questions and get evidence-based educational answers. Cites sources. Never diagnoses. Always routes to professional care when needed.

population.title: Population Health
population.desc: Anonymized population health insights for researchers, public health officials, and policymakers. Screening rates, risk prevalence, healthcare gaps.

conditions.title: Thailand's Most Critical Health Conditions
conditions.subtitle: 71% of deaths in Thailand are caused by non-communicable diseases. Most are preventable or detectable early. Start here.
conditions.viewAll: View all conditions in our Disease Library

diabetes.name: Type 2 Diabetes
diabetes.desc: Thailand has one of the highest diabetes rates in Southeast Asia. Early detection through simple blood glucose testing can prevent serious complications.

hypertension.name: Hypertension
hypertension.desc: The "silent killer" — most people have no symptoms. Regular blood pressure monitoring is the most important preventive action.

cervical.name: Cervical Cancer
cervical.desc: Nearly 100% preventable with HPV vaccination and Pap smear screening. Thailand's low screening rate (under 30%) creates a major prevention gap.

liver.name: Liver Cancer
liver.desc: Strongly linked to Hepatitis B and C (endemic in Thailand) and liver flukes from raw freshwater fish consumption.

cardiovascular.name: Cardiovascular Disease
cardiovascular.desc: Heart attack and stroke account for the most deaths in Thailand. 80% are preventable with early detection and lifestyle modification.

colorectal.name: Colorectal Cancer
colorectal.desc: 90% survival rate when caught early vs. 10% when caught late. Colonoscopy screening starting at age 50 is highly effective.

trust.title: Built on Medical Evidence. Reviewed by Experts.
trust.subtitle: In healthcare information, trust isn't optional — it's the product. Every architectural decision on Health Compass is designed around earning and maintaining that trust.

evidence.title: Evidence-Based
evidence.desc: Every medical claim is graded using the GRADE methodology (A-D). Only systematic reviews, RCTs, and validated guidelines make it onto Health Compass.

reviewed.title: Medically Reviewed
reviewed.desc: All content is reviewed by licensed medical professionals — physicians, specialists, and public health experts — before publication.

updated.title: Regularly Updated
updated.desc: Clinical guidelines change. Our content refresh cycle ensures every piece of health information reflects the current standard of care.

cited.title: Always Cited
cited.desc: We show our work. Every claim links to its primary source — published guidelines, peer-reviewed studies, or authoritative health organization statements.

privacy.title: Privacy by Design
privacy.desc: PDPA and GDPR-compliant architecture. Your health data belongs to you. No selling. No sharing without explicit consent. Full data deletion on request.

noDiagnosis.title: Never Diagnoses
noDiagnosis.desc: Health Compass is a navigation and education platform. We explicitly and persistently disclaim diagnostic intent. We connect you to care — we don't replace it.

a.label: Strong
a.desc: Multiple high-quality RCTs or systematic reviews

trust.partnersLabel: Aligned with national & international health standards

riskPreview.title: Know Your Risk Before Disease Knows You
riskPreview.subtitle: Health Compass uses validated medical calculators to give you a clear picture of your personal health risks — not generic statistics, but your numbers, based on your profile.

sample.label: Sample Result — Educational Demo
sample.name: Diabetes Risk Example
sample.disclaimer: This is an educational example. Your real results depend on your personal health profile.
sample.interpretation: Your diabetes risk score indicates a high probability of developing Type 2 Diabetes within 10 years. This warrants immediate action.

globalExpansion.title: Built for Thailand. Designed for the World.
globalExpansion.subtitle: Every architecture decision supports 100+ languages, 100+ countries, and 1 billion users. We start local. We think global.

infra.multiRegionDesc: Supabase + Vercel Edge Network across AP, EU, US regions
infra.localizationDesc: Country-specific guidelines, insurance systems, care pathways
infra.privacyDesc: PDPA, GDPR, HIPAA-inspired. Data residency per country

cta.title: Your Health Journey Starts Today
cta.subtitle: Don't wait for symptoms to tell you something is wrong. Take control of your preventive healthcare now. It takes less than 5 minutes to understand your risk.
cta.footnote: Free forever · No credit card · PDPA compliant · Never sells your data

footer.disclaimerText: Health Compass provides health education and navigation information only. This is not medical advice and does not replace consultation with a qualified healthcare professional. Always seek physician advice for medical conditions.
footer.mission: Our mission is to eliminate preventable suffering through better health navigation. Evidence-based. Globally scalable. Locally relevant.
footer.copyright: © 2026 Health Compass. All rights reserved. Thailand · Southeast Asia · Global.

disclaimer.short: Educational information only — not medical advice
disclaimer.full: Health Compass provides health education and navigation information only. This is not medical advice and should not replace consultation with a qualified healthcare professional. Always seek the advice of your physician for any medical conditions.
disclaimer.educationalDesc: These risk assessments are educational tools based on validated medical calculators. They are not medical diagnoses. Results should always be discussed with a qualified healthcare professional.

diseases.title: Disease Library
diseases.subtitle: Evidence-graded health information on 500+ conditions. Every fact sourced, medically reviewed, and regularly updated.
diseases.searchPlaceholder: Search conditions, symptoms, or ICD-10 codes...
diseases.sectionSubtitle: High-burden conditions for the Thai population, based on MOPH data

diabetes.name: Type 2 Diabetes
diabetes.desc: A chronic condition affecting how the body processes blood sugar (glucose). 40% of cases are undiagnosed.

hypertension.name: Hypertension
hypertension.desc: High blood pressure — the most common and preventable cardiovascular risk factor globally.
hypertension.keyFact: Often has no symptoms until serious damage occurs

cervical.name: Cervical Cancer
cervical.desc: Cancer of the cervix, strongly linked to HPV infection. Nearly 100% preventable with vaccination and screening.
cervical.keyFact: Pap smear screening reduces mortality by 80%

liver.name: Liver Cancer
liver.desc: The most common cancer in Thai men, strongly linked to Hepatitis B/C infection and liver flukes.

colorectal.name: Colorectal Cancer
colorectal.desc: Cancer of the colon or rectum. 90% survival rate when caught early via colonoscopy screening.
colorectal.keyFact: Stage I survival: 90% vs Stage IV: 10%

depression.name: Depression
depression.desc: A common and serious mood disorder affecting 1 in 7 Thais. Highly treatable with proper support.

riskPage.title: Understand Your Health Risks
riskPage.subtitle: Validated medical calculators that give you a clear, personalized picture of your health risks — with actionable next steps, not just numbers.

cvd.title: Cardiovascular Risk
cvd.subtitle: Framingham 10-Year CVD Risk
cvd.desc: Calculate your 10-year risk of heart attack or stroke. Includes blood pressure, cholesterol, smoking, and diabetes factors.

diabetes.title: Diabetes Risk
diabetes.subtitle: FINDRISC — Asian Adapted
diabetes.desc: Assess your Type 2 Diabetes risk using the internationally validated FINDRISC score, adapted for Thai and Asian populations.

cancer.title: Cancer Risk Profile
cancer.subtitle: Multi-Cancer Screening Tool
cancer.desc: Personalized cancer risk profile covering breast, colorectal, cervical, and prostate cancer based on validated screening guidelines.

mental.title: Mental Health Check
mental.subtitle: PHQ-9 + GAD-7
mental.desc: Validated screening instruments for depression (PHQ-9) and anxiety (GAD-7) symptoms. Always routes to professional support.

riskPage.diabetesSectionSubtitle: Complete the assessment below to get your personalized diabetes risk score.
riskPage.aboutDesc1: FINDRISC (Finnish Diabetes Risk Score) is one of the most widely validated diabetes risk tools globally, developed by the Finnish Diabetes Association.
riskPage.aboutDesc2: This version has been adapted for Asian populations using WHO Asia-Pacific BMI thresholds (overweight ≥23, obese ≥27.5) which better reflect metabolic risk in Thai and Southeast Asian populations.
riskPage.validationRef1: • Lindström J, Tuomilehto J. Diabetes Care. 2003
riskPage.validationRef2: • WHO Expert Consultation on BMI cutoffs for Asian populations. 2004
riskPage.validationRef3: • Aekplakorn W, et al. Diabetes Research Thailand. 2006

screening.title: Your Personalized Screening Plan
screening.subtitle: Based on Thailand's national health guidelines. Know exactly what screenings you need, when to get them, and where to go.
screening.personalizedDesc: Complete your health profile to get a screening schedule tailored to your age, sex, family history, and risk factors.
screening.guidelineNoteTitle: Based on Thailand National Health Guidelines
screening.guidelineNoteText: Screening recommendations are based on the Thailand Ministry of Public Health (MOPH) guidelines, National Health Security Office (NHSO) Preventive Care Package, and Royal College of Physicians guidelines. Guidelines are reviewed annually and this planner is updated accordingly.

bloodGlucose.name: Fasting Blood Glucose
bloodGlucose.frequency: Every 3 years if normal; annually if pre-diabetic
bloodGlucose.guideline: MOPH Thailand · NHSO Preventive Care Package 2025

bloodPressure.name: Blood Pressure Screening
bloodPressure.frequency: Every 2 years if normal; more frequently if elevated

papSmear.name: Pap Smear (Cervical Cancer)
papSmear.for: Women aged 25–65 who are or have been sexually active
papSmear.frequency: Every 3 years (Pap smear) or 5 years (HPV co-test)

cholesterol.name: Cholesterol Panel (Lipid Profile)
cholesterol.for: Adults 35+ (men), 45+ (women), or younger with risk factors
cholesterol.frequency: Every 5 years if normal; annually if elevated

diabetes.title: Diabetes Risk Assessment
diabetes.subtitle: FINDRISC Score (Asian Population Adapted) · ~2 minutes
diabetes.exerciseDaysHelper: Days with at least 30 minutes of moderate physical activity
diabetes.familyHistoryHelper: Parent, sibling, or grandparent with Type 2 Diabetes
diabetes.disclaimer: This assessment is for educational purposes only. Results are not a medical diagnosis. Always consult a qualified healthcare professional.
diabetes.resultDisclaimer: This result is for educational purposes only and is not a medical diagnosis. Please consult a qualified healthcare provider to confirm your risk and discuss next steps.
diabetes.sourceValue: International Diabetes Federation, adapted for Asian populations

interpretation.low: Your current risk for developing Type 2 Diabetes is low. Maintaining your current healthy habits is the best protection.
interpretation.moderate: Your risk for Type 2 Diabetes is slightly elevated. Lifestyle improvements can significantly reduce this risk.
interpretation.high: You have a moderate risk of developing Type 2 Diabetes. A fasting blood glucose test is recommended.
interpretation.veryHigh: You have a very high risk. There is a strong chance you may already have undiagnosed Type 2 Diabetes or pre-diabetes. Please seek medical evaluation promptly.

actions.bloodTestDesc: A simple blood test can detect pre-diabetes or diabetes. Available at most hospitals and clinics.
actions.exerciseDesc: Aim for 150 minutes of moderate exercise per week. Even 30-minute daily walks significantly reduce diabetes risk.
actions.dietDesc: Replace white rice with brown rice or vegetables. Limit sugary drinks, including fruit juices.
actions.weightTitle: Discuss Weight Management with Your Doctor
actions.weightDesc: Even a 5-7% weight reduction can cut diabetes risk by up to 58%.
